questions.text: I believe my personal dreams matter less than the collective good.
I am often the last to know about interpersonal drama in my social circles.
My body keeps score of every emotional event — I feel the accumulated weight.
I hold the view that the moral concern I feel for those in pain is the most authentic expression of who I am at my core.
I believe that following a process faithfully is more reliable than relying on inspiration.
I notice when someone's body language contradicts their words.
When someone raises their voice near me, my whole system goes on high alert.
I hold the view that emotional distance during a crisis is what allows me to think clearly enough to actually be useful.
I often take the initiative to resolve ambiguity for others.
I find that I set the emotional and strategic tone in groups I join.
I am attuned to the silent negotiations that happen before a group makes a decision.
I find that my emotional responses don't tend to snowball or escalate.
I feel comfortable in ambiguous situations where no one has taken charge yet.
I feel that the degree to which I prioritize addressing suffering is the most transparent window into my real values.
I feel most productive when I have the freedom to shift direction at any moment.
I tend to schedule personal time before committing to obligations for others.
I believe the world does not need more self-interested people—it needs more givers.
I keep a detailed calendar and refer to it multiple times a day.
